name: GolfCode360 Studio
about: GolfCode360 Studio is an indoor golf training facility in Orange County, California, with two locations in Brea and Irvine. It specializes in biomechanics and physics-based golf instruction using advanced technology for swing analysis. The venue offers a structured educational approach to developing a repeatable, high-performance swing, suitable for both amateur and professional golfers. Appointments are required, with same-day availability possible, and all lessons must begin with a signed waiver.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to state-of-the-art launch monitors and 3D motion capture technology
Revolutionary biomechanics and physics-based teaching approach
Structured accelerated learning programs for rapid improvement
Focus on creating a repeatable, high-performance swing

simulator_hardware.notes: Primary launch monitor for shot data and simulation
Software and system for advanced ball tracking and analysis

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you should bring your own golf equipment. The studio does not explicitly mention club rentals for on-site use, though they offer club fitting services.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food or drink services. The venue appears to be focused on instruction and technology, suggesting outside food and drink may not be permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify an exact time for playing 18 holes. Session times depend on the type of lesson or booking, which are structured around individual instruction rather than full rounds.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The studio has instructors with experience coaching junior golf and offers programs suitable for juniors and youth. However, the structured, technology-focused environment may be more appropriate for older children and teens.
